mechanism_narrative: Rising federal funds rates increase borrowing costs and compress equity valuations, generating negative forward S&P 500 returns over the next month.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns show positive mean during rate increases with p-value less than 0.05, or Rank IC exceeds 0.05 in the opposite direction.

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate exceeds 5.0 percent, labor market slack suppresses corporate earnings growth expectations, driving negative S&P 500 returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: Forward 60-day returns are positive in more than 55 percent of months where UNRATE exceeds 5.0, or signal loses predictive power with Rank IC below 0.02.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous expansion in retail sales and housing starts signals broad consumer demand strength, which directly lifts corporate revenues and produces positive S&P 500 returns over the next week.
failure_condition: Joint positive deviations in RSAFS and HOUST fail to predict positive 5-day returns with accuracy below 50 percent, or interaction term coefficient is statistically insignificant.

mechanism_narrative: Declining industrial production during a monetary tightening cycle indicates supply-side contraction that outweighs demand, causing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: INDPRO declines during tightening periods yield positive 20-day returns in more than 60 percent of cases, or regime-conditional IC drops below zero.

mechanism_narrative: Sustained growth in total nonfarm payrolls reflects expanding economic activity and rising corporate profit margins, generating positive S&P 500 returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: 60-day forward returns are negative in more than 55 percent of months following payroll increases, or rolling Rank IC remains below 0.01.

mechanism_narrative: When the consumer price index rises above 205.0, inflation expectations accelerate and force tighter financial conditions, producing negative S&P 500 returns over the next month.
failure_condition: 20-day returns are positive in more than 60 percent of observations where CPIAUCSL exceeds 205.0, or threshold model fails to outperform baseline with p greater than 0.05.

mechanism_narrative: Divergence between rising real GDP and falling industrial production signals inventory buildup and weak downstream demand, leading to negative S&P 500 returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: Positive GDPC1 growth combined with negative INDPRO growth predicts positive 60-day returns in more than 55 percent of quarters, or interaction effect is insignificant.

mechanism_narrative: Rising unemployment during a post-shock recovery phase indicates fragile labor demand that undermines consumer spending forecasts, driving negative S&P 500 returns over the next week.
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns are positive in more than 60 percent of weeks where UNRATE rises during recovery phases, or regime-specific IC is negative.
